message:
perf(mvcc): gate CAS-attempts histogram off version-chain append hot path

`record_cas_attempt(n)` was called from `VersionStore::publish` on
every version-chain append and unconditionally did two relaxed atomic
`fetch_add`s — one on `FSQLITE_MVCC_CAS_ATTEMPTS_TOTAL` and one on a
bucketed histogram. No production path consumes the histogram
(`cas_metrics_snapshot` / `reset_cas_metrics` are diagnostic-only,
mirroring the snapshot-read histogram landed behind a gate in
bc4fa6b5), so those atomics were dead weight on the publish hot path
that sits directly downstream of every committed write.

Wrap the recording behind `MVCC_CAS_METRICS_ENABLED: AtomicBool`
(default `false`), mirroring `MVCC_SNAPSHOT_METRICS_ENABLED`:

- `set_mvcc_cas_metrics_enabled(bool)` / `mvcc_cas_metrics_enabled()`
  wrappers.
- `record_cas_attempt` is now `#[inline]` and bails on one relaxed
  bool load when off; the two atomics move into a
  `#[cold] #[inline(never)]` `record_cas_attempt_slow`.

The existing `cas_metrics_recording_buckets_progress` test flips the
gate on before recording so its histogram assertions still hold.

Before: 701 ns/publish (cas_metrics enabled — the pre-commit production state)
After:  659 ns/publish (cas_metrics disabled — the new default)
Delta:  -6.0% per version-chain append, paired n=9 trials, 7 of 9
        trials have cas_off faster than the matching cas_on sample.
Bench:  bench_publish_cas_metrics_gate (added here; 32 pages,
        20000 publishes/trial, toggles the gate within a single run
        to isolate its contribution from the visibility-ranges gate
        landed in f2707d1a)
Cmd:    cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc --lib --release \
        bench_publish_cas_metrics_gate -- --ignored --nocapture

Stacked on the visibility_ranges gate from f2707d1a, the combined
publish hot path savings since ec87700b landed are 2582 → ~659 ns/publish
range in synthetic benches (benches measure different publish shapes,
so those numbers are not directly comparable — this commit's isolated
delta is the -6.0% line above).

Correctness: observability CAS tests pass
(`cas_metrics_recording_buckets_progress`,
`cas_metrics_first_attempt_ratio_helper`) and the handful of
`invariants::tests` touching publish continue to pass.

Bead: bd-7cqb0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
